<a name="S3File"></a>
## type [S3File](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L22-L47>)

S3File represents an open S3 object for reading or writing. For read mode, data is streamed directly from S3 with range request support. For write mode, data is buffered in memory and uploaded on Close\(\). S3File is safe for concurrent use \- all operations are mutex\-protected.

```go
type S3File struct {
    // client is the minio client used for S3 operations.
    client *minio.Client
    // bucket is the S3 bucket name.
    bucket string
    // key is the S3 object key (path within the bucket).
    key string
    // reader is the underlying minio object reader for read operations.
    reader *minio.Object
    // offset is the current read position within the object.
    offset int64
    // size is the total size of the object (cached for SeekEnd).
    size int64
    // writeMode indicates if this file was opened for writing.
    writeMode bool
    // writeBuffer accumulates write data until Close() uploads it.
    writeBuffer *bytes.Buffer
    // mu protects concurrent access to file state.
    mu  sync.Mutex
    // config holds S3 configuration for advanced features.
    config S3Config
    // useMultipart indicates whether to use multipart upload.
    useMultipart bool
    // multipartWriter is used for streaming multipart uploads.
    multipartWriter *MultipartWriter
}
```

<a name="newS3File"></a>
### func [newS3File](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L114>)

```go
func newS3File(client *minio.Client, bucket, key string, write bool) *S3File
```

newS3File creates a new S3 file handle for the given bucket and key. Deprecated: Use newS3FileForReading or newS3FileForWriting instead. This function is kept for backward compatibility with existing tests.

<a name="newS3FileForReading"></a>

```go
func newS3FileForReading(client *minio.Client, bucket, key string) *S3File
```

newS3FileForReading creates a new S3 file handle for reading from the given bucket and key. Data is streamed from S3 on Read calls with optional range request support.

```go
func newS3FileForReadingWithConfig(client *minio.Client, bucket, key string, config S3Config) *S3File
```

newS3FileForReadingWithConfig creates a new S3 file handle for reading with configuration.

```go
func newS3FileForWriting(client *minio.Client, bucket, key string) *S3File
```

newS3FileForWriting creates a new S3 file handle for writing to the given bucket and key. Data is buffered in memory and uploaded to S3 when Close\(\) is called.

<a name="newS3FileForWritingWithConfig"></a>
### func [newS3FileForWritingWithConfig](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L94-L98>)

```go
func newS3FileForWritingWithConfig(client *minio.Client, bucket, key string, config S3Config) *S3File
```

newS3FileForWritingWithConfig creates a new S3 file handle for writing with configuration.

<a name="S3File.Close"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L316>)

```go
func (f *S3File) Close() error
```

Close closes the S3 file and releases resources. For write mode, uploads buffered data to S3 using PutObject or multipart upload. For read mode, closes the underlying reader if open. Close must be called to ensure data is persisted for write operations.

<a name="S3File.EnableMultipartStreaming"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [EnableMultipartStreaming](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L409>)

```go
func (f *S3File) EnableMultipartStreaming(ctx context.Context) error
```

EnableMultipartStreaming enables streaming multipart upload mode. In this mode, data is uploaded as parts during Write\(\) calls instead of buffering. This is useful for very large files that exceed available memory.

<a name="S3File.Read"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [Read](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L126>)

```go
func (f *S3File) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Read reads data from the S3 object into p. The first Read call opens a connection to S3 and streams the data. Subsequent reads continue from where the previous read left off. Returns io.EOF when the end of the object is reached.

<a name="S3File.ReadAt"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [ReadAt](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L245>)

```go
func (f *S3File) ReadAt(p []byte, off int64) (n int, err error)
```

ReadAt reads len\(p\) bytes starting at offset off using S3 range requests. This is more efficient than Seek\+Read for random access patterns. ReadAt does not affect the current file offset.

<a name="S3File.ReadAtContext"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [ReadAtContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L514>)

```go
func (f *S3File) ReadAtContext(ctx context.Context, p []byte, off int64) (n int, err error)
```

ReadAtContext reads data at offset with context support.

<a name="S3File.ReadContext"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [ReadContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L438>)

```go
func (f *S3File) ReadContext(ctx context.Context, p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

ReadContext reads data with context support for cancellation.

<a name="S3File.Seek"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [Seek](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L187>)

```go
func (f *S3File) Seek(offset int64, whence int) (int64, error)
```

Seek sets the offset for the next Read operation. Seeking is only supported in read mode. Seeking closes the current reader; a new one opens on the next Read.

<a name="S3File.Stat"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [Stat](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L301>)

```go
func (f *S3File) Stat() (FileInfo, error)
```

Stat returns file info for this S3 object by calling HeadObject on S3.

<a name="S3File.Write"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [Write](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L173>)

```go
func (f *S3File) Write(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Write writes data to the S3 object buffer. Data is accumulated in memory and uploaded when Close\(\) is called. Returns an error if the file was opened for reading.

<a name="S3File.WriteAt"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [WriteAt](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L272>)

```go
func (f *S3File) WriteAt(p []byte, off int64) (n int, err error)
```

WriteAt writes data at the specified offset in the write buffer. Note: S3 does not support partial writes. All data is buffered in memory and uploaded as a single object when Close\(\) is called. For sparse writes, zero\-byte padding is inserted to fill gaps.

<a name="S3File.WriteContext"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [WriteContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L490>)

```go
func (f *S3File) WriteContext(ctx context.Context, p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

WriteContext writes data with context support for cancellation.

<a name="S3File.calculateOffset"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [calculateOffset](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L217>)

```go
func (f *S3File) calculateOffset(offset int64, whence int) (int64, error)
```

calculateOffset computes the new offset based on whence and current position. For SeekEnd, fetches object size from S3 if not already cached.

<a name="S3File.openReader"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [openReader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L149>)

```go
func (f *S3File) openReader() error
```

openReader initializes the S3 object reader with optional range request. If offset \> 0, uses an HTTP Range header to resume from that position.

<a name="S3File.openReaderWithContext"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [openReaderWithContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L469>)

```go
func (f *S3File) openReaderWithContext(ctx context.Context) error
```

openReaderWithContext initializes the S3 object reader with context support.

<a name="S3File.upload"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [upload](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L340>)

```go
func (f *S3File) upload() error
```

upload uploads the buffered write data to S3 using PutObject or multipart upload. Called internally by Close\(\) when there is data to upload. Uses multipart upload for data exceeding the multipart threshold.

<a name="S3File.uploadMultipart"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [uploadMultipart](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L393>)

```go
func (f *S3File) uploadMultipart(ctx context.Context) error
```

uploadMultipart uploads data using multipart upload for large files.

<a name="S3File.uploadSimple"></a>
### func \(\*S3File\) [uploadSimple](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_file.go#L359>)

```go
func (f *S3File) uploadSimple(ctx context.Context) error
```

uploadSimple uploads data using simple PutObject.

<a name="S3FileSystem"></a>
## type [S3FileSystem](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L25-L32>)

S3FileSystem implements FileSystem for Amazon S3 and S3\-compatible stores. It uses the minio\-go client library for all S3 operations. S3FileSystem supports path\-style and virtual\-host\-style bucket addressing.

```go
type S3FileSystem struct {
    // client is the underlying minio client for S3 operations.
    client *minio.Client
    // config holds the S3 configuration used to create this filesystem.
    config S3Config
    // regionCache caches detected regions for buckets.
    regionCache sync.Map
}
```

<a name="NewS3FileSystem"></a>
### func [NewS3FileSystem](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L39>)

```go
func NewS3FileSystem(_ context.Context, config S3Config) (*S3FileSystem, error)
```

NewS3FileSystem creates a new S3 filesystem with the given configuration. The context parameter is currently unused but reserved for future use. Returns an error if the minio client cannot be created.

<a name="NewS3FileSystemWithClient"></a>
### func [NewS3FileSystemWithClient](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L55>)

```go
func NewS3FileSystemWithClient(client *minio.Client, config *S3Config) *S3FileSystem
```

NewS3FileSystemWithClient creates a new S3 filesystem with a pre\-configured client. This is useful for testing with mock clients or for sharing clients across filesystems. If config is nil, uses default S3 configuration values.

<a name="S3FileSystem.Capabilities"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [Capabilities](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L341>)

```go
func (*S3FileSystem) Capabilities() FileSystemCapabilities
```

Capabilities returns the capabilities of this filesystem. S3 supports seek \(via range requests\), range reads, directory listing, write, and delete operations. Append is not supported.

<a name="S3FileSystem.Create"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [Create](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L186>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) Create(path string) (File, error)
```

Create creates an S3 object for writing. Data is buffered in memory and uploaded when Close\(\) is called.

<a name="S3FileSystem.CreateConcurrentReader"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [CreateConcurrentReader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L529-L532>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) CreateConcurrentReader(ctx context.Context, path string) (*ConcurrentReader, error)
```

CreateConcurrentReader creates a concurrent reader for efficient large file reads.

<a name="S3FileSystem.CreateContext"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [CreateContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L498>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) CreateContext(ctx context.Context, path string) (File, error)
```

CreateContext creates an S3 object for writing with context support.

<a name="S3FileSystem.CreateMultipartWriter"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [CreateMultipartWriter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L516-L519>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) CreateMultipartWriter(ctx context.Context, path string) (*MultipartWriter, error)
```

CreateMultipartWriter creates a streaming multipart writer for large uploads. This is more efficient than buffering the entire file in memory.

<a name="S3FileSystem.Exists"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [Exists](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L311>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) Exists(path string) (bool, error)
```

Exists checks if an S3 object exists. Uses HeadObject and checks for NoSuchKey or NotFound error codes.

<a name="S3FileSystem.GetClient"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [GetClient](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L682>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) GetClient() *minio.Client
```

GetClient returns the underlying minio client. This can be useful for advanced operations not exposed through the FileSystem interface.

<a name="S3FileSystem.GetConfig"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [GetConfig](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L676>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) GetConfig() S3Config
```

GetConfig returns the current S3 configuration.

<a name="S3FileSystem.Glob"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [Glob](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L357>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) Glob(pattern string) ([]string, error)
```

Glob expands a glob pattern to a list of matching S3 object keys. Uses ListObjectsV2 with prefix optimization to efficiently list objects. Handles pagination automatically for buckets with more than 1000 objects. Applies retry logic with exponential backoff for rate limiting.

<a name="S3FileSystem.GlobContext"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [GlobContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L362>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) GlobContext(ctx context.Context, pattern string) ([]string, error)
```

GlobContext expands a glob pattern with context support for cancellation.

<a name="S3FileSystem.MkdirAll"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [MkdirAll](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L197>)

```go
func (*S3FileSystem) MkdirAll(_ string) error
```

MkdirAll is a no\-op for S3 since directories are implicit. S3 uses key prefixes as virtual directories.

<a name="S3FileSystem.Open"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [Open](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L175>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) Open(path string) (File, error)
```

Open opens an S3 object for reading. Returns a File that streams data from S3 on Read calls.

<a name="S3FileSystem.OpenContext"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [OpenContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L480>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) OpenContext(ctx context.Context, path string) (File, error)
```

OpenContext opens an S3 object for reading with context support.

<a name="S3FileSystem.ReadDir"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [ReadDir](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L271>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) ReadDir(path string) ([]DirEntry, error)
```

ReadDir reads the contents of an S3 "directory" \(objects with a given prefix\). Uses ListObjects with Recursive=false to list only direct children.

<a name="S3FileSystem.ReadDirContext"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [ReadDirContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L571>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) ReadDirContext(ctx context.Context, path string) ([]DirEntry, error)
```

ReadDirContext reads directory contents with context support.

<a name="S3FileSystem.Remove"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [Remove](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L221>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) Remove(path string) error
```

Remove removes an S3 object. Returns an error if the delete operation fails.

<a name="S3FileSystem.RemoveDir"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [RemoveDir](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L239>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) RemoveDir(path string) error
```

RemoveDir removes an S3 "directory" \(all objects with a given prefix\). Lists all objects with the prefix and deletes them one by one.

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) Stat(path string) (FileInfo, error)
```

Stat returns file info for an S3 object. Uses HeadObject to retrieve metadata without downloading the object.


<a name="S3FileSystem.URI"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [URI](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L334>)

```go
func (*S3FileSystem) URI() string
```

URI returns the base URI for this filesystem. Always returns "s3://" for S3 filesystems.

<a name="S3FileSystem.detectBucketRegion"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [detectBucketRegion](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L610>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) detectBucketRegion(ctx context.Context, bucket string) (string, error)
```

detectBucketRegion detects the region for a bucket using the GetBucketLocation API. Results are cached to avoid repeated API calls.

<a name="S3FileSystem.detectRegionFromHead"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [detectRegionFromHead](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L639>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) detectRegionFromHead(ctx context.Context, bucket string) (string, error)
```

detectRegionFromHead tries to detect bucket region from a HEAD request error response. This is a fallback method when GetBucketLocation is not available.

<a name="S3FileSystem.listObjectsWithPrefix"></a>
### func \(\*S3FileSystem\) [listObjectsWithPrefix](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_filesystem.go#L407-L410>)

```go
func (fs *S3FileSystem) listObjectsWithPrefix(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, pattern string) ([]string, error)
```

listObjectsWithPrefix lists S3 objects using the given prefix and filters by pattern. Handles pagination automatically for buckets with more than 1000 objects. Uses retry logic with exponential backoff for transient errors.

<a name="S3URLStyle"></a>
## type [S3URLStyle](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/s3_config.go#L12>)

S3URLStyle represents the URL style for S3 bucket addressing.

```go
type S3URLStyle string
```

<a name="S3URLStylePath"></a>

```go
const (
    // S3URLStylePath uses path-style addressing (e.g., https://s3.amazonaws.com/bucket/key).
    S3URLStylePath S3URLStyle = "path"
    // S3URLStyleVirtual uses virtual-host-style addressing (e.g., https://bucket.s3.region.amazonaws.com/key).
    S3URLStyleVirtual S3URLStyle = "virtual"
    // S3URLStyleAuto automatically selects the URL style based on the endpoint.
    S3URLStyleAuto S3URLStyle = "auto"
)
```

<a name="SegmentType"></a>
## type [SegmentType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/glob.go#L273>)

SegmentType represents the type of a pattern segment.

```go
type SegmentType int
```

<a name="SegmentLiteral"></a>

```go
const (
    // SegmentLiteral is a literal path segment with no wildcards.
    SegmentLiteral SegmentType = iota
    // SegmentWildcard is a segment with *, ?, or [...] wildcards.
    SegmentWildcard
    // SegmentRecursive is the ** recursive wildcard.
    SegmentRecursive
)
```

<a name="factory"></a>
## type [factory](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/filesystem/factory.go#L28-L31>)

factory is the default implementation of FileSystemFactory.

```go
type factory struct {
    mu      sync.RWMutex
    schemes map[string]FileSystemFactoryFunc
}
```

<a name="defaultFactory"></a>defaultFactory is the global default factory instance.

```go
var (
    defaultFactory     *factory
    defaultFactoryOnce sync.Once
)
```
